---
title: The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA)
alias: &ALIAS tcga
href: https://www.cancer.gov/ccg/research/genome-sequencing/tcga
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) is a landmark cancer genomics program that molecularly characterized over 20,000 primary cancer and matched normal samples spanning 33 cancer types. The project generated comprehensive, multi-dimensional maps of the key genomic changes in major types and subtypes of cancer.

## Key Features

- **Multi-Omics Data**: Genomic, epigenomic, transcriptomic, and proteomic data
- **33 Cancer Types**: Comprehensive coverage across major human cancers
- **Open Access**: Data available through GDC (Genomic Data Commons)
- **Matched Controls**: Primary tumors paired with matched normal tissue
- **Clinical Annotations**: Detailed patient clinical data and outcomes

## Use Cases in Research

- Cancer genomics and mutation analysis
- Biomarker discovery and validation
- Drug target identification and pharmacogenomics
- Tumor microenvironment characterization
- Survival and outcomes research

## Getting Started

Visit [GDC Data Portal](https://portal.gdc.cancer.gov/) to access and download TCGA data.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
